entry.title: Re: Use of elements not permitted in CDS view Data Defini...

entry.title: Re: How i create a BR to change AddressOrigin
entry.content:
Hello Aziz,

I personally don't have experience with this change but I would say that you need to change activity.address to new ID after equipment is changed. I really can't see the logic to change activity.address.object, that field does not exist on object Activity. If you was you, I would change variable "address" to address.object = equipment.id and then to action put "address" = ${address.id}. In action Update object you put only the field name, so "ac.address.object" is incorrect.

Filip

entry.title: Re: Delete a row based on a condition in Adobe form
entry.content: Not at Form Level(i.e. JavaScript or FormCalc) , delete the row at the source only that is your Driver Program.

entry.title: How to track the person responsible or custodian of the asset in sap s4hana public cloud
entry.content:
Client wants to track the asset, who is responsible for the asset .

For example,

If a company distributed laptop to a employee, how can i track the person responsible for asset in sap s4hana public cloud.

entry.title: IW38 change main work centers mass change
entry.content: Is it possible to change all the main work centers to an diffrent main work center with an mass change? Please let me know.

entry.title: Email templates not aligned how to fix that? learning plan notification for learner
entry.content:
In Launch Content editor I added some content & image while I added & saved the data and I did not do any changes in Loops, but when I send the preview all the main content is reflecting behind that. 

Added content only in the top but not anything I am adding its going at the back & not showing anything on that & it added extra spaces automatically, how can we fix it so that the content added should reflect on top.

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....
entry.content:
Have you checked the format parameters in the set variant of program SAPFPAYM for the payment medium format?

This setting overrides the settings in Format.

entry.title: Re: Posting CxML Invocie from postman with PDF attachment...
entry.content: Hi Prem. Thanks for the response. We have done posting cxml invoice from postman and invoice reaching sucessfully. now facing challange to post cxml invoice with PDF attachment

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Mobile Services Native/MDK App Authorization...
entry.content:
Mobile Services is a generic collection of services to provide a common set of interfaces for your mobile applications. MDK is one type of application that can be created. You can also build a pure native application using the SAP BTP SDK for iOS or Android that uses Mobile Services.  As a broader generic collection from the Security tab it provides multiple options an application can utilized. 

Even though there are other security options that Mobile Services supports, the MDK client has decided to only supports OAuth2 at this point in time.  As this functionality is currently not available in MDK, I would suggest you to open a feature request here. We'll review it based on its priority.

entry.title: Re: Status change not recorded in Action Log after BADI implementation
entry.content:
Do you actually want to replace the current user id and current date/time already saved in the action log?

  • 'Current Date', 'Time' and 'User Name'

entry.title: Re: Local ATC Checks fail with incorrect message "Execution of check seemingly aborted"
entry.content:
The note 3357888 - Check failures duing ATC check with check variant SLIN_SEC - SAP for Me says:

The cryptic message will be improved to: There is no Code Vulnerability Analyzer (CVA) license in the system. See SAP Note 1855773 for more information.

1855773 - Security checks for customer-specific ABAP programs - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: How to find the tabstrip ??
entry.content: Hi, Did you find a solution yet?

entry.title: Re: Purchase order & Purchase Requisition Release Strateg...
entry.content:
Hello @HARSHILVAGHELA495 

Indeed the currency conversion should work as indicated in the note. If it doesn't put a breakpoint in the include LEBNDF01 around line 441, where currencies are converted before actual strategy determination:

If that doesn't work as expected, report the issue to SAP support.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Extending Activity Object in SAP FSM to Display Related Emails and SMS
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have a requirement to extend the Activity object in SAP FSM to display all emails and SMS that were generated (via business rules) for a specific case to the customer and technician.

I am aware that these messages can be found in the Admin section under "Messages", but I am struggling to retrieve the message ID from the URL (e.g., cb65664d-ba5b-4746-be76-a6a5eefbe99b).

Additionally, I am not certain if it is even technically possible to create a connection between the Activity and the corresponding message.

Has anyone encountered a similar requirement or has experience with this? Is it technically possible to connect Activity / ServiceCall objects to messages? Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

Filip

entry.title: Re: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001....
entry.content: Did you already check KBA 3455390 - ISO20022 pain.001.001.09 XML-based Message Format Is Missing for Netherlands  (even if not in NL)

entry.title: Use of Program rfopenposting_fr in New GL for FEC filing
entry.content:
Hi,

My client has migrated this year from classic GL to New GL. Now, they have a requirement to submit the FEC file for previous 3 years. As per the process suggested by SAP below sequence need to be followed:

1. Run Program RFOPENPOSTING_FR with Tcode SE38 or Tcode FAGL_FR_03 to populate Balances

2. FTW1A for extraction

3. FTWH for execution and generation of FEC file.

Now, I need to know if

a. the first step needs to be carried for last 3 years (2022, 2023 and 2024) individually?

b. Also, as they were on classic GL for last 3 years and now on new GL, will this program read balances in new GL correctly?

Regards,

Poonam

entry.title: Re: Close Group Tree button is hidden in Crystal report v...
entry.content:
I sent this off to R&D and he confirmed it's a regression issue since SP 36.

So they will fix it in SP 39, no time line for the next SP though...

Thanks for reporting it.

Stay on SP 35 for now....

entry.title: Are you looking for learning materials for the Travel & Expense solution area?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Travel & Expense Solutions learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey is designed for you to discover the Travel & Expense solution area. 

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to understand the fundamental aspects of the Travel & Expense solutions and their potential. You will learn about SAP’s cloud strategy, the integrated portfolio, and how it connects to end-to-end business processes and industries.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Back Order Processing - Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP in S/4HANA) version 2023 Part II

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/advanced-available-to-promise-aatp-in-s-4hana-version-2023/ba-p/13857242

Can you now check?

Regards,

Hari

entry.title: Re: How to restrict authorization for accounting document reversal (FB08) and reset cleared item (FB
entry.content:
Hi @Sungwhan,

I am not a FIN guy and cannot give you a direct answer.

On the other hand, I suggest you thinking differently: you want to limit the viewing access to the accounting document.  How do you view them in the Public Cloud? Find out the apps or actions doing that, and put a restriction on them. 

On-premise and Cloud solutions are designed differently, and you need to take a different approach to tackle the same problem sometimes.

Regards,

George

entry.title: How to schedule job for open & close posting period with dynamic date for multiple countries
entry.content:
Hi Team,

My want schedule jobs for open & close periods for multiple countries with dynamic date for each month (4+4+5 Week). Please advise if it's possible in SAP S/4HANA Public edition cloud or not, if yes how we can achieve business requirement.

Regards

Pravin Binit

entry.title: Re: Use of Program rfopenposting_fr in New GL for FEC filing
entry.content: Didn't your company/customer already generate some Opening Balances and FEC extraction for the 3 last years in classic GL (this file should be mandatory since 2014?)

entry.title: Re: Sending Email notifications for Time Valuation Alerts
entry.content:
Hi @marcelo28  - Please check the API visibility field in your custom MDF object definition. It should be set to editable . Post this please perform the ODATA Metadata refresh

Then it should be visible in the IC

I haven't experienced the same. What Web Browser do you use? Have you tried a different brand Web Browser, like Chrome or Internet Explorer? 

Also, can you pin point which app does that more often than others?

Regards,

Geore

George

entry.title: Outbound Delivery archival
entry.content:
Hello Experts
I am new to EWM
I am trying to archive the outbound deliveries from 2018-2020 in my project but the problem here is the outbound deliveries are completed but the TU status of the deliveries are not correctly reflecting and in the FDO its still showing in Transit status, also the set to archive status is not active for those deliveries
Can someone help me here

TIA
Jyotsna

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
We have a strong demand in the Middle East for ECP, also because of the DC in the UAE and KSA, where the demand of all customers (driven by the government) is to have personal data and especially payroll data in the country.

While we embrace this initiative, it will raise many questions and might stop deals in these countries due to personal data leaving the country. What is the plan for these customers? Do you have a timeline for when GCP in KSA and Moro in UAE will be supported?

entry.title: How to assign value to oApplication object
entry.content:
Hi

  I have below code . How to assign value to oApplication object

Hi

  I have below code i am getting error - Object reference not set to an instance of the Object.

oApplication.MessageBox("Sap B1 Connected");

SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany = new SAPbobsCOM.Company();
oCompany.Server = "10.0.1.28:30013";
oCompany.DbServerType = BoDataServerTypes.dst_HANADB;
oCompany.CompanyDB = "LIVE";
oCompany.UserName = "manager";
oCompany.Password = "123";
oCompany.language = BoSuppLangs.ln_English;
oCompany.UseTrusted = true;

int connectionResult = oCompany.Connect();
if (connectionResult != 0)
{
    throw new Exception("Error connecting to SAP Business One: " + oCompany.GetLastErrorDescription());
}

oApplication.MessageBox("Sap B1 Connected");
C#
Copy

Thanks

Thank

entry.title: How to display the first processor of a ticket in an SAP C4C report?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working with SAP C4C Service and would like to display the first processor of a ticket in a report. My goal is to see who handled the ticket first.

What I have tried so far:

Creating a new report based on the "Tickets"data source.

Searching for a field like "First Processor" / "Initial Processor", but I couldn't find it directly.

My questions:

Is there a standard field in C4C that stores the first processor of a ticket?
If not, is there a way to achieve this using a custom data source ?
Does anyone have a solution or best practice to make this information visible in a report?

Thanks in advance for your support!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer inside UpdatePanel displays onl...
entry.content:
Hello, did you manually change the code or did you let VS generate it?

R&D tested this and noted there is a difference.

"I tried VS2019 with SP39 and it did work.  (note: SP 39 is in testing)

But my aspx showed a little different code:"

    <form id="form1" runat="server">
        <div>
        </div>
        <asp:ScriptManager ID="ScriptManager1" runat="server">
        </asp:ScriptManager>
        <asp:UpdatePanel ID="UpdatePanel1" runat="server">
            <ContentTemplate>
                <CR:CrystalReportViewer ID="CrystalReportViewer1" runat="server" AutoDataBind="True" GroupTreeImagesFolderUrl="" Height="1200px" ReportSourceID="CrystalReportSource1" ToolbarImagesFolderUrl="" ToolPanelWidth="200px" Width="1104px" />
                <CR:CrystalReportSource ID="CrystalReportSource1" runat="server">
                    <Report FileName="Grouping and Sorting.rpt">
                    </Report>
                </CR:CrystalReportSource>
            </ContentTemplate>
        </asp:UpdatePanel>
    </form>

for clarity your code, it is quite different:

<asp:ScriptManager ID="ScriptManager1" runat="server">
</asp:ScriptManager>
<asp:UpdatePanel ID="UpdatePanel1" runat="server">
<ContentTemplate>
<div id="ReportViewerContainer" style="width: 100%; height: 95vh;">
<CR:CrystalReportViewer ID="CrystalReportViewer1" runat="server" AutoDataBind="true" HasRefreshButton="True" HasCrystalLogo="False" HasToggleParameterPanelButton="False" ToolPanelView="None" EnableDatabaseLogonPrompt="False" HasDrillUpButton="False" HasExportButton="False" HasPrintButton="False" BestFitPage="False" Width="100%" Height="100%" />
</div>
<asp:Button ID="ApplyFormula" runat="server" Text="Button" style="display:none" OnClick="ApplyFormula_Click" />
</ContentTemplate>
</asp:UpdatePanel>

entry.title: error detected by export dll
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a problem exporting to Excel data only format. Error detected by export dll. I have a version of crystal reports 11.5.3.417. My operating system is windows 11 pro. When I export to Exel format it doesn't fail, it only fails when I export to Excel data only format. In the report there are some fields of SQL expressions type. If I remove any of these expressions then it doesn't fail. But the expressions are very simple, they are of the type (select field from table1).

How can I resolve the error?

thanks.

entry.title: Re: Badge for SAP BDC learnings?

Hi @ChemaEscolar 
The  Introducing SAP Business Data Cloud does not include a record of achievements
The Positioning SAP Business Data Cloud includes a record of achievement. 
You need to pass all the quizzes to receive a digital badge.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Application is crashing after some time while exporti...
entry.content: Not for this, you woul dneed to add your own logging component, CRLogger does log database calls but I doubt it would capture anything for the crash. DebugDiag, from Microsoft may catch more details.

entry.title: How to change Document type via BAPI using Alternative Document Type 1/2 like in VA02
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

 

I have some difficulty to find how to change the document type of an existing sd document using a BAPI. I found this solution to enable the modification in VA02 but I'm not able to replicate it through abap code ..

 

Any idea ?

entry.title: Error on lest than 35h rest over weekend
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to trigger an alert/warning if an employee rests less than 35 hours over the weekend. I have successfully configured an error for less than 11 hours of rest between working days using the following Time Valuations:

  1. TV 1 – Captures working time in a Time Container.
  2. TV 2 – Captures start and end time of the rest period using Create Time Record valuation type with a Time Type Group containing working time from TV 1.
  3. TV 3 – Captures gaps between working days by deducting working time (from TV 1) from the rest period (from TV 2).
  4. TV 4 – Defines the minimum rest period using Fixed Clock Time (9 AM - 8 PM).
  5. TV 5 – Captures rest rule violations by comparing the minimum rest period (from TV 4) with gaps between working days (from TV 3).

Could anybody please share their expertise or input on how to configure a 35-hour minimum rest period over the weekend? I'm unable to define the minimum rest period.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,
Aesha
SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

entry.title: Re: Deployment of Organizational Structure from Developme...
entry.content:
Hi @adnanmaqbool ,

It is a fair question. We usually explain this topic in an architecture overview, what is the purpose of each tenant, and how CBC configuration impacts them, and the transport system setup. However, there is no guardrail to limit you on how many orgs you can create for the Development Project in CBC, because it really depends on your use case. 

On the other hand, when you deploy your CBC configurations to the Development Tenant, there is no transports created for you to release/export these configurations to the Test Tenant and Production Tenant. This is a kind of hard guardrail to prevent you from moving development org configurations to the T and P tenants. From Development Tenant, you can only create and export development transports (ABAP codes) to the downstream tenants.

Hope this explanation helps. 

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: Integration Odata V4 Service in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Found the Service with slightly different name

entry.title: Hi, I am not able to subscribe to SAP Build Apps using trial account. It gives failed error.
entry.content: It gives failed error, anyone got luck with loading it successfully?

entry.title: MTA App with UI5 - Running same application isolated in one subaccount / multiple spaces
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have CAP MTA Application with UI5 and Typescript. It's hosted in one space and published in WZ Standard. Now i want to isolate the application as an stable version for demo purposes and in the same subaccount but different space follow up development to different S4HC backend and a different code (another branch of the project). 

The idea is doing it totally automatic. Tried with mta extension descriptor, using placeholders as {space}, the app manifest.json, etc... but the wz just detect one application. 

Any clue?? That's possible?

Thank you so much! 

PS. Is not the same as multitenancy, cause the app at the end "is not the same"

entry.title: Streamline Data Migration from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: Selective Data Transition with Time-Slice
entry.content:
Enhanced Selective Data Transition Capabilities with Time-Slice

On top of the possibility to leave behind outdated company code data, the selective data transition scenario in SAP BTC now includes further capabilities to reduce the data scope. These enhancements allow for even more precise data selection, ensuring that only the most critical and relevant data is migrated. This not only optimizes the data transition process but also support compliance with data retention policies and regulatory requirements.

What is Time-Slice?

Time-Slice is a powerful feature within SAP Business Transformation Center's Selective Data Transition scenario that allows organizations to selectively transition data from the legacy SAP ECC system based on a specific key date. This means you can choose to migrate only the most relevant and recent data, leaving behind outdated or unnecessary information such as inactive company codes or fiscal years from the past. By doing so, you not only reduce the volume of data being transferred but also enhance the efficiency and speed of your SAP S/4HANA data migration projects.

SAP Business Transformation Center allows to reduce the amount of data moved from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA based on the dimensions company codes & fiscal years to reduce the data footprint as shown below:

 

Read the documentation about the time-slice solution pattern.

Watch this video, to get the full picture of the time-slice solution pattern!

 

Benefits of Using Time-Slice

Reduce your Data Footprint by transitioning only the necessary data, you can significantly decrease the amount of data stored and managed, leading to cost savings and improved system performance.

Data Footprint Reduction by using Time-Slice & Company Code Selection

 

 

 

Total Data in Scope

- No Company Code Selection

- No Time-Slice Selection

 

0% reduction*

Total Data in Scope

+ Company Code Selection

- No Time-Slice Selection

 

32% reduction*

Total Data in Scope

+ Company Code Selection

+ Time-Slice Selection

 

77% reduction*

*Based on a real live example of a leading manufacturing company

Enhance your Data Quality by focusing on recent and relevant data ensures that your migrated data is accurate and up-to-date, reducing the risk of errors and inconsistencies. Same can be verified using integrated Data Transition Validation (SAP DTV) tooling. 

Migrate Faster with a smaller data set to transfer, the migration process becomes quicker and more efficient, minimizing downtime and disruption to your business operations.

Improve the Compliance asselective data transition helps in adhering to data retention policies and regulatory requirements by ensuring that only the required data is retained and migrated.

 

How Time-Slice and Selective Data Transition Work

The Time-Slice feature in SAP BTC is designed to be user-friendly and easily customizable. Here’s a step-by-step overview of how it works:

1. System Analysis: Conduct a thorough analysis of your existing data to identify the relevant time periods and data sets for migration using SAP Readiness Check for Usage and Data Profiling.

In Manage Digital Blueprints app, in the solution pattern section for “Time-Slice”, data insights into the data usage across the different years are conducted. This provides an idea of the system age and data used in the previous years.

2. Define Scope: With the analysis file attached, use the Manage Digital Blueprints app to create a Digital Blueprint and to specify the time periods and company codes, that need to be transitioned. Furthermore, attach a system scan to include also additional tables such as custom tables into the migration scope). In the solution pattern section for time-slice in the Manage Digital Blueprints app, the user has the possibility to define the fiscal year where the data should be taken from:

 

3. Create a Transformation Model: Set up the Transformation Model based on your scoping exercise covered in the Digital Blueprint to create the technical foundation of your migration. The Transformation Model delivers pre-defined standard content for the selective data transition including filtering for company codes and time-slice.

4. Create a Transformation Model Version, based on your Transformation Model which is a frozen “ready-to-execute” technical foundation for your data migration.

5. Create Migration Cycles: Set up migration cycles based on the Transformation Model Version that contains the defined scope, which is ensuring a smooth selective data transition for only the selected company codes and fiscal years.

6. Setup Data Validation (optionally): Use the integrated data transition validation (DTV) tool to compare the data of the source system with the data in the target system after load.

7. Run the Data Migration: Walk through the execution steps in the Manage Cycles app to execute Data Transformation: Perform the Prevalidation, Preparation, Transformation Execution and the Post Processing.

 

Real-World Success Stories

Many organizations have already noticed the benefits of using Time-Slice in SAP BTC. For instance, a leading manufacturing company can reduce its data footprint by 77% and cut down migration time by 50%, all while maintaining data integrity and compliance. Due to the intuitive and standardized guided data migration process, overall data migration planning, implementation and execution effort is very low compared to similar projects in the past.

Conclusion

The Time-Slice feature in SAP Business Transformation Center is a game-changer for businesses looking to optimize their data migration processes. By selectively transitioning data, you can reduce your data footprint, enhance data quality, and achieve faster, more efficient migrations. Embrace the power of Time-Slice and take your data management strategy to the next level with SAP Business Transformation Center's Selective Data Transition scenario.

 

 

Further information

E2E Documentation

E2E Product video demonstrating SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA lean selective data transition (without time-slice feature)

Time-Slice Feature Video

Other blogs: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-business-transformation-center-lean-selective-data-transition/ba-p/13931763

entry.title: Re: How to reset system user password in HANA database?
entry.content:
Please check:

  • 3501306 - How To - Reset the SYSTEM User Password in the System Database
  • 2274157 - How to reset SYSTEM user password in a Tenant Database
  • 2554177 - How To Reactivate SYSTEM User
  • 2736167 - How to unlock/reactivate the SYSTEM user on SAP HANA?

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: C_HAMOD example question
entry.content:
Hi, in the Exam sample questions for C_HAMOD this question is contained with the stated answer:

I dont't see why "only USA" is correct. When we query the sales CV, then the income view and its priviliges shouldn't matter at all. In my opinion, "Europe and USA" should be correct.

Can anybody explain why "only USA" is correct?

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Everything about using SAP Business Transformation center for SDT S/4 HANA Conversion 2023 from

@rajarajeswari_kaliyaperum Thanks for the great blog! Today I released a blog about our newest feature - the time-slice solution pattern:  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/streamline-data-migration-from-sap-ecc-to-sap-s-4hana-selective-data/ba-p/14018508

This allows to not only leave outdated company codes behind but also fiscal years no longer needed. Might be interesting for the users reading your blog, too.

entry.title: Re: Badge for SAP BDC learnings?
entry.content: Dear Margit, I passed all the steps and quizzes, and digital badge was not issued.

entry.title: Re: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content:
I see a "Request a quote" button, which is missing in your screen print, next to the "Download now" button (see attached).  This is the case when USA and Canada countries are selected.  Are you selecting another?  

Nonetheless - this is a change.  There used to be a "Buy Now" button, and I have sent customers to buy SQL Anywhere this way.  

If you can work with an OEM version of SQL Anywhere, authenticated to a particular application, you can buy licenses from a reseller such as Novalys.  

I'm surprised you have had no other replies.  It seems forum activity has really dropped off since moving to this new SAP forum platform.  

I'll throw in a related message, while I'm here: It's clear SAP has little enthusiasm for SQL Anywhere, which came with the Sybase acquisition.  Many people, including myself, have overwhelming enthusiasm for the truly excellent and underappreciated software that is SQL Anywhere.  If so, SAP, please divest SQL Anywhere to Appeon as you did with PowerBuilder.  The PowerBuilder spinoff turned out wonderfully for everyone, and I have no doubt the same would be the case for SQL Anywhere as well.  Many companies depend on SQL Anywhere, including, no doubt, many large SAP ERP customers, and companies that could become SAP ERP customers.  Why not create goodwill with an action that would not be much trouble, with a company that you have a track record with, while unburdening yourself of software that is not core to your mission in the process?

entry.title: Re: How to schedule job for open & close posting period w...
entry.content:
Hi Raymond,

We have already checked but did not find any solution as period closing date are dynamic each month.

Regards

Pravin Binit

entry.title: Re: Upgrade impact to roles 2502
entry.content:
I have two blogs specifically written to address this topic:

  • Review and Adapt Business Roles after a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Review Business Role Changes before a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Responsible for creating an inbound delivery in SAP S/4
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are implementing SAP S4 HANA, I have a question regarding an inbound process.

Inbound deliveries will be created in S4/HANA automatically based on EDI, what if the EDI information is not available? Is that a purchaser's responsibility to create inbound delivery or who from the business should be responsible?

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hello Maurizio, 

CALM is triggering the tp commands like tp import to the TMS of the managed landscape.

In the ULOG file you will see an entry like this one when you trigger the import - DEploy from a Feature with only one TR like  S4HK900XXX into S4H:300:

APServiceS4H 2025... RFC: tp IMPORT S4HK900XXX S4H clires300 pf=\\...\sapmnt\trans\bin\TP_DOMAIN_S4H.PFL FEEDBACK AFTERIMP -Dimpmon_mode=DETAILED -DSYSTEM_PF=

But if you trigger the import Deploy for one Feature that contains several TRs or from several Features, then you will see in ULOG an entry like:

APServiceS4H 2023... RFC: tp IMPORT SUBSET S4H pf=\\...\sapmnt\trans\bin\TP_DOMAIN_S4H.PFL FEEDBACK AFTERIMP -Dimpmon_mode=DETAILED -DSYSTEM_PF=\\...\sapm
SUBSET means in detail: S4HK900042 300 S4HK900064 ..... 300 (pid=14044)

Then TMS will be then in charge to import the included transport request in the SUBSET in the order that they have in the import buffer of that target system client, meaning the released order.

From CALM we have not chance to change the import order when triggering a tp import subset.

Another key point is that if the import of a TR ends with an TMS error, from the CALM Feature we will not trigger anymore the import of this TR, you will get a Set Repair status button next to it.

Meaning that you will need to do whatever you need to do to get the TR imported into that target system manually at TMS level, and then set this TR as repaired in the Feature.

So, if you really feel that you need to change the order in the import buffer of a target system, the only option that you have is to remove to the import queue and add again to get the "desired" import order in that target system:client. 

There is not other option here.

I hope this answer your question. 

Best regards,
Dolores

entry.title: Re: CDS Analytical Projection Views - the new Analytical Query Model
entry.content:
@Kishore

@AndreaUS 

thanks for sharing the details. At the moment this feature is not supported in analytics. But you could create a requirement for this such it might be developed in the future.

Regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: QM-IDI versus Inspection Lot API in LIMS integration
entry.content: Hi, Can you please provide steps on how to Integrate SAP and LIMS using API instead of QM-IDI

entry.title: Comlemento de Pago de Factoraje con DRC?
entry.content:
Hola equipo, Actualmente con DRC eDocument obtengo los complementos de pago de los clientes, tengo un escenario de factoraje el cual incluye dos nodos de pago en la estructura del XML requerida por el SAT. Alguna idea de manejo de este escenario o de incluir el segundo pago mediante la ampliación edoc_adaptor?

Gracias de Antemano

Martín

entry.title: How to implement the usage tracking using SAP IT plugin in fiori
entry.content:
I am trying to enable some usage tracking and importing in the SAP IT library but I think I am not doing it correctly 

Log-dbg.js:497  2025-02-17 13:44:42.213699 [FUTURE FATAL] Failed to load component for container container - ModuleError: Failed to resolve dependencies of 'sample-project/Component.js'
-> 'sapit/util/UsageTracking.js': failed to load 'sapit/util/UsageTracking.js' from resources/sapit/util/UsageTracking.js: script load error  
ModuleError: Failed to resolve dependencies of 'sample-project/Component.js'
-> 'sapit/util/UsageTracking.js': failed to load 'sapit/util/UsageTracking.js' from resources/sapit/util/UsageTracking.js: script load error

 

using the following as a guide: SAP IT Usage Reporting | SAP IT Cloud

Here is my code

entry.title: Re: Manage Projects with material procurement in S/4 Publ...
entry.content:
Hi Stefano,

Yes, it is possible to manage material procurement for projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.  

Please check the links below for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/302bbfc4cc0a498aae4cef4256046cf6.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2502.500

Thanks,
Sreevidya

Marcus

entry.title: Issue data blending in Story using table
entry.content:
I have two different Models in SAC and I want to combine this two model in a story and create a table visualization. 

Both Models has been linked by a common dimension member avaialbe in both models. Everything is working properly when using  a Chart in the story. But NOT more for Table. 

Could some one support me?

Best regards,

Linking 2 Models in Story by using Table is not working

 

Linking 2 Models in Story by using Chart is working

Just brain off easy to read comedy and entertainment. Fun fact I read the main characters voices as Marty and dr. Emmett from back to the future, which makes it even better for myself.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to enable Single-Sign On ( SSO ) to lo...
entry.content:
Hey, I have a follow-up question.
Is it possible to manage a user's permissions in the admin console from the Corperate IDP (in our case Microsoft). For example by defining certain attributes?
Or can permissions only be set through the admin console -> administration tab?
Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: JBOSS 8 Compability
entry.content:
There is currently no such thing as JBoss 10.

JBoss 8 is the latest and current version.

I think the documentation is confused and is actually referring to Wildfly 10 - A very old version of wildfly. (Wildfly is sort of the FOSS version of JBOSS )

Currently we are using JBoss 7.4 and that maps to Wildfly 23

We are about to move to JBoss 8.0 and that maps to Wildfly 28

The latest version of Wildfly is 35

entry.title: Re: Manage your hierarchies in FIORI 1/3
entry.content:
Hello Marcus,

 

we are using mostly

0COSTELMNT_0102_HIER, 0COSTCENTER_0101_HIER, 0PROFIT_CTR_0106_HIER, 0GL_ACCOUNT_T011_HIER and 0GLACCEXT_T011_HIER.

Just make sure to replicate the corresponding hierarchies into the HRRP-tables. 

 

Regards,

Kamil

entry.title: Re: Change of position name doesn't update in IT0001

BR

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi Dolores,

I was not sure that I could modify manually import buffer order because in the official documentation these 2 recommendation are reported: 

- We recommend avoiding manual import activities in CTS with transports managed by SAP Cloud ALM.

- Do not perform buffer manipulations in CTS with transports managed by SAP Cloud ALM. There is no self-healing mechanism available.

But if it is still possible as reported in your answer, if necessary I'll try to change the buffer order manually.

Regards

 Maurizio

entry.title: Re: Freight Cost Allocation and Service Entry Sheet not generated while posting the Freight Accrual.
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,
it should usually not happen, that only parts of the document chain is created during accruals posting. If an error occurs during posting, the freight document should be displayed in the Errors section of the Monitor Accrual Posting app and an application log with error messages should be available. Can you please check if this is the case and provide more detailed information.
With best regards,
Thomas Rösch
SAP SE

entry.title: SAC - Data locks on this model prevented the changes from publishing (data action script, one user)
entry.content:
Hello experts.

I'm facing a very frustrating issue with a data action script transferring records from a model to another.

Only one user has the error message below : 

The results of the data action "abcde" could not be published on version "2025_B1". You can still try to do a partial publish manually. Data locks on this model prevented the changes from publishing.

While for several other users the script runs fine.

(of course parameters are the same in each run)

Data locks are obviously managed the correct way (if not the case all runs would fail except for the admin user).

My user (with the planning professional licence) has of course no issue.

What could be different for user XXXX ? What would explain this difference of results ?

(I am considering opening a support ticket).

Any relevant investigation tricks would be welcome.

Thanks in advance & Regards.

G.

Now we have the issue that the created country hierarchie does not appear in Manage Allocation App as receivers parameter.  

Do you have a recommendation, what to do next? 

Thanks and regars

Sebastian B.

entry.title: Re: can we set up workflow when an order/ quote goes on c...
entry.content:
Hi @Ganeshmasne,

You can set up workflows for sales documents, but these are relevant for specific fields as sales organization, sales order type and net value: Approval Workflows for Sales Documents

However, if SAP Credit Management is active, you need to follow the standard process to release the document: Manage Documented Credit Decisions

I would recommend exploring the available features of SAP Credit Management.

Hope this helps.

Arturo

entry.title: Re: I need to make crystal report group by category verti...
entry.content:
Hello,

Put a group on category. Show your category in group header.
And show activity in detail's section.

You can underlay the header or show category on details.

You always have 3 activity by category ?
It's for excel's export ? 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: error detected by export dll
entry.content:
You stated that "If I remove any of these expressions then it doesn't fail.".
Did you mean to say If I remove all of these expressions then it doesn't fail?

Can you replicate the problem with a simple report using the Xtreme sample database?
If so, please upload that rpt file.  Add a .txt extension to allow the upload.

entry.title: Determination of the newest = latest record for non-effective dated objects
entry.content:
Abstract

There is often the request to fetch the latest record from non-effective dated records, as e.g. the highest educational qualification, the latest previous employer or the last performance rating given to an employee. There is no standard functionality to derive such details in one shot but there is a workaround which is presented in this blogpost in detail.

Introduction

As long as an object is effective dated, it is trivial to derive in a report the currently valid record, e.g. the current job title from job information or the marital status of an employee from personal information. However, there are many data which are stored in non-effective dated tables and objects. Examples are:

  • Education data with the school/university, a start date and end date together with the respective qualification (e.g. bachelor, master or doctoral degree)
  • Data about the previous employments with the employer name, start date and end date of the previous employment, together with the activities and tasks in the respective previous companies
  • Annual performance ratings based on performance and objective evaluations (typically, valid from 1 January until 31 December of the respective year)

In many reporting use cases, however, it is required to derive just the latest (=youngest) record and not the complete history. Figure 1 shows as an example the calculated overall performance ranking of “Andree Durant” (in that system, the 2017-record is the latest=youngest record).

Figure 1 – Talent profile data of sample employee Andree Durant

Figure 2 shows the simple report output, which is based on a query that uses “Basic User Information” and the performance forms. You see that the complete history is shown, i.e. 6 records for the years 2012 until 2017 with the respective “Calculated Overall Performance Ratings”. The reporting challenge is deriving just the latest performance rating, i.e. 2,89 valid for 2017; so that the report shows just a single row for employee “Andree Durant”. This blogpost describes the process how to achieve that. Of course, the approach can also be used for other cases (e.g. those mentioned in the introduction above or any other).

Figure 2 – Report output of a simple query based on “Basic User Information” and the performance forms

Solution

In the following the required steps are described to get just the latest record instead of the full history.

  1. Creation of the query, e.g. “Basic User Information” -> “Form” -> “Performance”. Select the fields which are relevant in your case, such as e.g. username, user ID, employees name, form start date / form end date and the “Calculated Overall Performance Rating”.
    Now create following calculated columns:
    1. Calculated column “DAYSBETWEENENDDATEANDTODAY - Days Between End Date and Today” as shown in the upper part of Figure 3.
      Important: Use “MIN” as ‘Aggregation Type” for this newly created calculated column (as indicated in the lower part of Figure 3).
    2. In addition, create a second calculated column “DUMMY - dummy” as indicated in the middle part of Figure 3 (an explanation why “DUMMY - dummy” is needed and how it is used follows below).

Figure 3 – Definition of the two query calculated columns

 

  1. Create a calculated measure of type “Restriction” called “Minimum Days (Constant Selection)” as indicated in Figure 4. In particular take case on the following:
    1. It is important to declare the measure as a “constant” (see red arrow in Figure 4).
    2. Unfortunately the declaration of a measure as “constant” is only possible for a “Restricted Measure”. That means it is required to add a filter criteria. Here, it is recommended to use a (dummy) condition which is always fulfilled. Exactly here, it’s recommended to use the calculated column “dummy” mentioned above, i.e. to use a filter on "dummy = "a" (see blue box in Figure 4).
    3. Use all fields used in the “Cross-tab” except the “User ID” and “Username” as “Constant Dimension” (see yellow box in Figure 4).

Figure 4 – Definition of calculated measure “Minimum Days (Constant Selection)”

As you can see in Figure 5, “Minimum Days (Constant Selection)” shows constantly the smallest value of the Day Difference for all records of the respective user.

Figure 5 – Report output with the previously created measures

As a final step, it is required to filter the records for the latest record, i.e. “latest” = record where “Days Between End Date and Today” is equal to “Minimum Days (Constant Selection)”. To achieve this, the following 3 steps are required:

  • Creation of the “Calculated Measure” called “Filter (Days = Min Days)”, see Figure 6
  • Conversion of the measure “Filter (Days = Min Days)” into a dimension “Filter as Dimension” as indicated in Figure 7: This step is needed since it is not possible to apply a filter in a “cross-tab” for a measure but only for a dimension.
  • Apply a filter to the “cross-tab” in the following way: “Filter as Dimension” Equals “1”

Figure 6 - Definition of calculated measure “Filter (Days = Min Days)”

Figure 7 - Definition of Measure-Based Dimension “Filter as Dimension”

 

Compare the outcome of the table as shows in Figure 8 with the expected outcome as indicated in Figure 2. You will realize that the outcome looks as expected.

Figure 8 – Report output after the filter “Filter as Dimension” = “1” is applied

As described in the beginning, of course, the same approach can also be applied for all other use cases when it’s needed to derive the latest record from non-effective dated records, e.g. to derive the highest educational qualification (in that case the query can be based on “Basic User Information” -> “Formal Education”) or to derive the previous employer (in that case the query might be based on “Basic User Information” -> “Previous Employment”).

Remark: This blog post was created with the support of Bastian Posch, Senior Service Advisor - Germany, HCM Cloud Services

entry.title: Re: How add custom field on XML/PDF on a eDocument for Ch...
The Metadata behind SAP Data Products in SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
On February 13th, 2025, we introduced SAP Business Data Cloud # (BDC)to help enterprises unlock value from their data, using it to power analytics, AI, and automation. Fundamental to BDC are Data Products: curated datasets that are accessible, self-describing, discoverable, and understandable . The value and usability of any data partly depends on the availability and quality of metadata, which is also true for the Data Products of BDC. This blog post provides a deep dive into the metadata for SAP Data Products.

In summary, SAP uses open metadata standards to describe Data Products. The API / data model of Data Products is described in Core Schema Notation Interoperability Specification (CSN Interop). The high-level information about Data Products, their taxonomy and relationships are described in Open Resource Discovery with a focus on taxonomy and connecting Data Products with development, integration, and business-oriented concepts.

 

High-Level Metadata

SAP developed a metadata discovery standard for describing and publishing various types of APIs and events: Open Resource Discovery (ORD). ORD is part of the Apeiro Reference Architecture and is contributed as open source to the European IPCEI-CIS initiative as a standard for interoperability. It was a natural step to leverage this existing standard rather than to create another isolated metadata standard that only covers the Data Product and platform aspects. So, we extended ORD to include the Data Product concept and connected it with other existing developer-focused concepts. 

This also helps with avoiding the typical problem of siloed and fragmented metadata graphs. We believe there is huge value in connecting the various metadata concepts that are relevant to developers, architects, data scientists, and business persona. Our strategy is to bring all these aspects together and aggregate the metadata into central repositories.

In our model, the Data Product is a grouping concept that carries the relevant metadata to describe the dataset and its data qualities. A Data Product itself is not directly consumable, but it exposes data through output ports, which can be implemented as APIs or events. In the first iteration of BDC, the primary Data Products expose a single output port as delta-sharing API. In principle, there can be multiple output ports of various API and/or event types. To understand data ingestion, lineage, and dependencies, Data Products can also have input ports which are the equivalent of an ORD Integration Dependency that lists the APIs and events that are used to ingest or process the data. Most of the concepts have relations to Entity Type, which represents business objects / domain objects / ODM entities as semantic taxonomy terms business experts would understand.

Right now, SAP uses the ORD protocol to streamline our own discovery and publishing processes, most notably to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) System Landscape, SAP Build and to the BDC Cockpit.  This all happens behind the scenes, so customers are not exposed to it and can benefit from a better integrated experience. However, in our partnership with Databricks, we also use the ORD protocol to connect the Databricks Unity Catalog with the Data Products provided by BDC as well as the other way around, retrieving Data Products created in Databricks into BDC. Leveraging ORD as an open metadata standard is a very important part of our partnership. We are looking forward to growing the ecosystem around ORD in our partnerships.

For more information about Data Products in ORD, have a look at the ORD Data Product page.

 

Data Product API / Data Model

Data Products exposed by SAP applications will use the industry-standard Delta Sharing protocol, but customers will also be able to expose Data Products via other types of APIs. In Delta Sharing, the Delta Table metadata format provides the basics for wider ecosystem interoperability. For many of our use cases, we need even richer metadata – more semantics and a model that is closer to our existing metadata models.

Many SAP products already support the SAP-specific Core Data Services (CDS) metamodel, which may also be known to our customers as the metadata format for ABAP (RAP) and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). Since we invested in rich metadata in CDS models for many years (sometimes decades), it made sense to leverage it also for Data Products, preserving and transferring the rich metadata and semantics we often already have.

As CDS models can be exported in the CSN format in many variants and flavors, we created a public specification that focuses on an interoperable, machine-consumption-optimized format: Core Schema Notation Interoperability Specification (CSN Interop).

With CSN Interop, we describe the API / data model of the Data Products output port. The most relevant aspects are that we describe entities (tables / objects) and elements (fields, properties). By assigning entities to a service, we define (or imply) the protocol and mapping how they are exposed as an API with a concrete data serialization format.

Since the CSN format is not well known outside of the SAP ecosystem, here are some interesting aspects:

CSN is well suited to describe “conceptual” models, which are more abstract than, e.g., JSON Schema. The same CSN model can be exposed through different API protocols and data serialization formats (via assignment to a Service). It can also be used as a persistency model for the database. The core entity model in CSN is therefore agnostic of protocol and serialization format. The final API / data interface is then decided by the chosen mapping. In the case of the Delta Sharing APIs, we defined a mapping to the Apache Spark type system.

Also, CSN already has extensive annotation vocabularies, which can carry a lot of additional information like business semantics (@Semantics, @ODM), relationships, and metadata that improve the semantic onboarding experience. This means that there is no need to rebuild a rich, semantic metamodel from scratch, because we can carry over the information. 

For more information, have a look at the CSN Interop page and its Primer.

 

Future Direction

We plan to extend the current scope of the metadata standards and further improve the data product metadata with more annotations and better coverage. We want to establish ORD as a de facto industry standard, being an intrinsic piece of our open ecosystem approach similar as we use ORD as the solid foundation of our partnership with Databricks.

If you have questions, feedback or just want to get in touch with us, feel free to reach out!

entry.title: Vendor changed into the partner function of Purchase order
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have the following problem, inside the ‘partner functions’ tab of the Purchase Order there is a different supplier from the one in the header of the document, as you can see in the attached photo:

 

The PO has no reference document. In addition, I have checked the business partner's master data and there is no link to the other supplier:

Fortunately, I checked in the report ‘Display Purchasing Documents by Supplier’ and there are no POs with the wrong supplier.
Only in the CDS View of the PO partners the wrong supplier appears; whereas in the CDS View of the document header, the only correct supplier appears.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAM auto-sync parameters
entry.content: Threshold determines how frequently autosync can run. So if your threshold is 30 and you save an item it will check if you have synced in the last 30 minutes. If you have, it will not sync. This is to keep the app from hitting the server more frequently than it needs to since it's very easy to rapidly do multiple save actions.

entry.title: Re: Create/Restore Postgres DB Backup
entry.content: Hi, can you try to run the restore for a different instance ? What is the service plan that you are using and please provide also information about the region where you are trying to do the restore. Regards Antonio

entry.title: Re: OAOA - Change AUC status from Investment Measure to N...
entry.content: I have the same issue

entry.title: Two cost lines same tax code split tax line
entry.content:
Hi,

our company code in China has the following request:

Vendor posting with FB01

Line 01: 34 Vendor   10.000

Line 02: 40 Cost GL  8.490,56 Tax code J3 6%

Line 03: 40 GL Cost 943,40 Tax code J3 6%

Line 04: 40 VAT GL 6% 566,04

The requirement is, to split the line 04 based on the values in line 02 and 03 - so we want to have instead one line on the tax GL in this case two lines with the same GL and same tax code. It should be an 1:1 relationship between the cost line and the tax line, so means for each cost line with VAT 6% we want to generate a new tax line with 6% in the same FI-document.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: How to create a simple SAP GUI using SAP Process Automation.
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

I am creating my first bot with Sales Order Creation, Unable to create my first automation.

 

regards

Madevan

entry.title: Re: SAM auto-sync parameters
entry.content: thanks for the reply, this makes sense as the PERIODIC parameter is a # not a Y/N flag so it handles the period itself

entry.title: Custom Message Mapping - TPM - B2B - SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In this Blog, I would like to share the effective way of using Message Mapping in B2B Scenarios using TPM and SAP Integration Suite.

Prerequisites:
Knowledge in SAP Integration Suite, Integration Advisor, TPM, Message Mapping, B2B Concepts.

Lets get Started:

TPM allows us to use a custom mapping instead of the standard MAG/XSLT mappings.

In case if you use the above approach, you have to create a Custom Integration Flow using Process Direct Sender with the address mentioned as above,

Normally what we tend to do is, create "N" number of Integration Flows because of either of the following reason,


1. You maintain different addresses in TPM for different agreements.

OR

2. You maintain the same address in TPM, but you have different message mapping for partners and EDI messages.

Here, What we will do is eliminate the number of Integration Flows created in this process, Instead we will try to achieve all B2B transformations through just one single Integration Flow.

To achieve this, Proceed as below:

Step 1:
Maintain a parameter in TPM agreement,

For each inbound/outbound agreement, provide an activity parameter.

Key: Put any Parameter Name of your choice but should be same for all B2B agreements. (Inbound/Outbound)

Value: Your Message Mapping ID value. (Reference shown below)

Step 2:
Create a package and create your Integration Flow.

Step 3:
Access the Custom Parameters of the Agreement using Groovy.

In this case:

Property Name <==> Activity Parameter Key
Property Value <==> Activity Parameter value (Refer Step 1)

Step 4:
Add "ref:" to Property Value

Step 5: 
Use this property in Message Mapping

Step 6:
Add Global reference to your Groovy and Message Mappings.

So next time, you onboard a new Customer / Vendor / 3PL, All you have to do is

A. Maintain agreements in TPM.

B. Create Message Mapping as per your need.

C. Add global reference of the message mapping into the Integration Flow (Created in Step 2).

PS: You can refer the Groovy scripts from Accelerator Hub,

Thanks for your time. 


Regards,

Kottiyil Ramnath

SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Cumulativ Material Ledger initialization (MR22)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Here is my problematic:
Our customer plans to use the material ledger in Year-to-Date Run Type at system startup:

The question we're asking ourselves today is this: How to initialize the Material Ledger value?

I mean, our customer has been calculating the actual production costs of his products for years and would like the Material Ledger to consider the last known value at start-up, before calculating the actual stock value (CKMLCP) at the 1st monthly closing in SAP. (the go-live takes place during the current fiscal year)

However, they does not wish to define this actual price as the standard price for their products.

If we had been using a S/4HANA On Premise system, we would have followed the following procedure:

Initialization scenario: (Go live on 01/03/2025)

  • Load material master data
  • Load stocks: Quantity and standard or MAP values at month-end (28/02/2025)
  • Validate inventory value, reconcile with balance sheet on inventory accounts (Business)
  • Run CK40N as of 01/03/2025 (new period) => Automatic generation of accounting entry for standard revaluation difference
  • 01/03: loading of actual price difference with transaction code MR22 on date 28/02/2025 to integrate current value
  • Run Material Ledger (CKMLCP) for period M-1 (February)

We are currently experiencing a problem with the step highlighted in red, which to our knowledge cannot be carried out in S/4HANA Public Cloud, as an application equivalent to the MR22 transaction code does not appear to exist.

The fioriappslibrary provides the Manage Material Valuations application for transaction code MR22. But this application doesn't seem to allow us to modify the stock value as the MR22 transaction did. (I mean debit/credit a material)
According to the online documentation for S/4HANA Public Cloud, this functionality should be available : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/0152bfb67de346768835bd7ccba3c07c.html?locale=en-US&version=2502.500

Are we missing something in app F2680?

We did some tests with API_MATERIAL_VALUATION_SRV (https://api.sap.com/api/API_MATERIAL_VALUATION_SRV/path/post_InventoryPriceChange) but it just changed the material price like a MR21.

Thanks for your support.

Romain C.

entry.title: Re: Use of Program rfopenposting_fr in New GL for FEC filing
entry.content:
Can you still see the previous opening balance documents after switching to the new GL (use FB03 as these documents should not be displayed with FAGLL03 / FAGLB03) So you should only have to generate the opening balance of 2025?

If postings have already been created for the target fiscal year, the system should issue a warning or error message (since 2021?)

entry.title: EDI_DOCUMENT_STATUS_SET requires EDI_DS which is forbidden in cloud
entry.content:
Hi.

I am trying to call EDI_DOCUMENT_STATUS_SET from BTP, so I defined the communication arrangement, communication services (for open, close and status_set) and now I hit a snag when writing the call.

In the coding above, ls_idoc_status should have type EDI_DS. If I try to define the variable, I get an error that EDI_DS is not allowed.

The POC coding I am working from used a Z version of EDI_DS, which basically copied everything over in the code, but.... really? Ok, for a POC this is ok, but surely for real production coding there must be a better solution than this. Even redefining it as an own DDIC object is... inelegant, I think.

What could I do here?
